segment-text: FRANCES HA Noah Baumbach & Greta Gerwig
IMAGES of Tompkins Square Park. Over the tableaus we
HEAR two women talking and laughing.
EXT. PARK. MORNING
We find Frances and Sophie, both 27, setting up a little performance space in front of a statue.
Sophie sings and plays a shitty guitar badly. Frances dances.
When people walk by, they both stop and yell:
Give us your MONEY!!!
They laugh. Sometimes someone will give them change. Usually a creepy guy.
(alone now) Give us-(laughs) Come on!
Give us your money!!!

Frances and Sophie run through the park together. They dump what little money they earned into another musician's guitar case. Frances makes sure to keep a dollar bill. They run into the subway. Subway. Sophie sits. Frances stands above her. leans down to say something. Frances cooks. Scrambled eggs and stir fry. Sophie enters frame and gets plates out of the cupboard. She
I tried a frittata but it's more of a scramble.
Frances is reading and Sophie is knitting.
Oh, this is interesting.

She reads a sentence out loud from "Sincerity and Authenticity" by Lionel Trilling. Sophie nods. Sophie works out to a video in the tiny living room. Frances half watches. Frances and Sophie smoke in two separate adjacent windows. They both lean outside. We see them talking outside the windows. They sit across from each other in their living room.
(doing a Jamaican accent) Okay, my father is from Jamaica, mon, and-(doing an Irish accent) --my mother is from Ireland. (trying to combine the accents) So I talk like this... They both crack up.
(doing a Russian accent) My mother is from Russia and-(doing a Southern accent) --and my father is from Alabama. (trying to combine) So I talk like this.

They play a game together. They do their laundry at the laundromat across the street. They both lie in bed and watch The Day After Tomorrow on the computer. Sophie falls asleep first and Frances quietly puts the computer away. Frances makes a move out of the bed.
(eyes closed) Don't be stupid.
I should sleep in my own bed. Why?
Because I bought it.
Stay. But take off your socks.
Okay...Thank you. Shut up.
CLOSE on a photo of a bony, bug-eyed cat that resembles an alien.
It's hairless? DAN (O.S.) No, it has fine curly hair.

CLOSE on Frances looking down. She looks up briefly, attempts a smile and looks back down. CLOSE on her hand touching the back of her neck. She stares at a photograph in her lap. She is wearing dance clothes underneath a knit dress, and her hair is pulled back. A bulky canvas backpack sits beside her. She'll have it the whole film. CLOSE on her fingers smoothing the skin on her arm.
(excited) There's this house in Westchester, the basement is all Cornish Rexes,
it smells awful, the top floor is Siamese cats. She nods and hands Dan, early 30's, back the photo. He sits opposite her on a thrift-store couch, a manilla envelope in his lap.
I'm allergic to cats.
They're hypo-allergenic. (quick smile) This is a special Frances kitty.
Oh...who knew?
He passes her back a photo.
This one is the runt. Which is cheaper but I think is the best one anyway. I figured one runt and one regular?
Frances hesitates.
I put a hundred dollar down payment on each cat...or eighty for the runt and a hundred for the other one.
Pause.
Why not get one cat?
A cat needs a buddy. If you get one cat you need to get two cats.
I'll give you two hundred dollars to get no cats.

He nods, somewhat hurt and annoyed, glances back at the photo of the runt, starts stacking the photos and tucking them back into the envelope. Frances comes over and sits next to him, puts her hand on his back.
So, who will take care of them if you go away?
I thought...we'd get them together.
And move them back and forth between our apartments?
No...I thought maybe you could move in with me...
Frances hesitates.
(strangely) Oh, wow...
Do you want to move in with me? Yeah. Yeah? Yes.
(pause) I mean, I do have this other thing.
What "other thing?"
Well...I don't know. I promised Sophie I'd stay through the lease.
He sighs deeply.
Is that bad? I'm sorry. bad.
I feel
Can't she find someone else?
Yeah. But, it's my friend... Uh huh.
I want to move in with you. Okay.
I feel bad.
Don't feel bad. Forget it.
No... (off his expression) What? Nothing.
You're mad.
I'm not mad. I'm disappointed.
I feel bad.
Stop feeling bad, Frances! I asked you to move in, you said, No.
Her phone rings.
But I can't.
You can, you don't want to.
She looks at her phone.
It's okay, I don't need to get it. Who is it?
It's Sophie. I'll call her back.
Just pick up the phone.
Do you mind?
(frustrated) Answer it.
She does.
(into phone, a voice) Hey girl...waz up wid you? Where you at?...I can't come now... (laugh) Are you drunk?...I can't...I love you...
Dan watches her without expression. a beer from the fridge. He gets up and gets
(laughs) Dumpling house!...I love you...I can't really talk now...I love you...Okay!...I love you.
She hangs up and looks at him somewhat sheepishly.
She's with Benji and Lev at this party in Chinatown. (pause) They're drunk.
He drinks his beer.
I don't know...maybe this isn't working. I'm sorry. Me too.
(a new idea) Maybe... (pause) Forget it. What?
I do want to pay you for the cats.
I'm not going to take money from you. But I'm going to get the cats anyway.
But then you're going to be a single guy with two cats.
Why...why am I going to be single?
With two cats.
No, I know. I mean why would I be single?
I don't know... I thought you said this wasn't working. I mean, if we don't move in together. If we can't move in together.
We can move in together.
I don't know...I don't know if I believe anything I'm saying.
Uh huh. (pause) This hasn't been great for a while.
(nods) This hasn't been great for a while. This has not...
She looks at him sadly. Frances puts on a too-big bomber jacket, not really cool, just something a friend gave her once.
Where are you going?
I guess home. I'm really tired. I'm always so tired.
She puts on her backpack.
(sadly) Let's move in together.
INT. APARTMENT PARTY. NIGHT

CLOSE on Frances (wearing the same clothes). She walks through a crowd and leans in to Sophie. Sophie is talking to Benji and Lev. WIDER: They drink, dance in a silly way, laugh. Lev plays a record. Frances. He tries very hard to flirt with
I can't get interested in art or books or movies about animals. Animals in conversation. The animals have to talk or be at war for me to be interested.
INT. SUBWAY STATION. NIGHT
No one is on the platform. Frances pulls down her pants and pees into the tracks, holding onto the pillar to suspend her bare bottom out over the track. Sophie looks for a train. She turns to see--
(laughing) Don't hit the third rail!
Sophie's attention goes to an MTA flyer posted.
Shit! The three's not running!
INT. CAB. NIGHT
Frances lying in Sophie's lap.
INT. SOPHIE/FRANCES APARTMENT. WINDOW SILL. NIGHT
Frances, in her same clothes, smokes a cigarette by the open window. Sophie, sleep-wear on, sits next to her with her legs propped up on the sill. Sophie extends her hand for the cigarette.
I want a sip.
Do you want your own?
I want a sip.
Frances reluctantly hands it over.
The coffee people are right. We are like a lesbian couple that doesn't have sex anymore.
Sophie hands it back and exits frame.
Wait! Come back!
Don't pick at your face.
(kind of to herself, touching a pimple) I don't like smoking alone.
She puts out her own cigarette and follows Sophie to the bedroom. Sophie is writing an email on her phone.
Me and Dan don't have sex anymore...didn't have sex anymore.
Yeah, that's why you broke up.
Or if it happened then he would just - he only had one way that he could finish.
I hate that.
Sophie enters the bathroom. Washes her face.
It was always with me facing flat on the bed from behind. Which just...everything that matters was covered up.
It's like with me and Patch -
(making fun) Yo Patchy!
(also making fun) 'Sup bra? (continue, matter of fact) The way he always likes to come in my face.
Yeah, CLASSY. Patch is the kind of dude who says "I gotta take a leak."
(cringing) And wears pre-distressed baseball hats. But...he's a nice guy...for today.
Frances joins Sophie in the bathroom.
I should feel bad that I went to the party.
You deserved it. You just got out of relationship jail.
I think Dan was an alcoholic. He was.
Sophie leaves frame.
He wasn't a real alcoholic. Frances leaves frame.
But sometimes he would have twelve beers.
Sophie lies in bed. She's still typing on her phone.
Stop playing on your phone.
Frances flops down into bed next to her.
It's the only thing with interweb in this house. I have to answer work emails.
(to herself) The neighbors changed their code. Hey!
(re: her phone) What?
Lev is asking for your number.
Give it to him!
(not judgemental) You did just break up with Dan today.
Just do it.
She types in the number. Frances gets her phone from a bag and stares at it. Waiting. Sophie goes back to typing on her phone. Frances' phone chirps. Reads out loud.
"Ahoy sexy. Are you around this week?" Ahoy sexy?
That is actually very gay.
Am I nautically sensual?
You're funny.
Lev didn't laugh at any of my jokes tonight.
You are SO funny.
Should I text back "Starboard anal sex?"
Sophie laughs. Puts her phone down. Snuggles down in bed. No.
(settling) Tell me the story of us...
Again? Well, Frances, we are going to take over the world...
...You'll be this awesomely bitchy publishing mogul...
...And you'll be a famous modern dancer and I'll publish a really expensive book about you...
...That d-bags we make fun of buy to put on their coffee tables...
...and we co-own a vacation apartment in Paris...
...and we will have lovers...
...and no children...
...and we will speak at college graduations...
...and honorary degrees!
...so many honorary degrees.
INT. BEDROOM. LATER.
Sophie and Frances are asleep in the same bed.
INT. DANCE STUDIO. DAY.
Frances warms up alone in an empty studio. While holding a position, Frances notices a woman pass by the open doorway.
INT. DANCE OFFICE. MOMENTS LATER.
Frances pops her head into the outer office. Hey!
Colleen, 40's, beautiful, retired dancer - drops everything she's holding. Jesus!
Sorry. I'm here.
I don't really have time to talk, I'm doing the job of three people here, but I can see you after rehearsal.
I read that article in the Times -you must be so happy!
Oh yeah, thanks, I forget I make my own work sometimes, but thanks.
I usually hate that woman's writing.
Right. Well, thank you Frances.
I actually, I had a couple of questions. I'm trying to be proactive about my life.
(getting it) I'll see if I have some studio
-- Uh, no. I wanted to know if there were any more classes at the school that I could teach...I'm a little, I'm kind of broke.
I'll check, but I'm all full I
-- I thought so, I'm just proud of myself for asking.
Well, we'll probably use you and some other apprentices for the Christmas show, so, that's something.
That's GREAT.
Frances just stands there.
I just wanted to say I really look up to you, and I just, I think you're great.
Ha! A beat-up old dancer doing paperwork?
Frances still stands there a little goofily. Colleen decides to wrap it up:
I really need to do all that paperwork now - I'm running rehearsal today so I'll see you in there.
Frances disappears.
INT. DANCE STUDIO. LATER
About twenty two dancers are rehearsing. Colleen runs the rehearsal. Frances is paired with Rachel, 25, about her height and build. They laugh and talk in between working.
Do you have more gum?
What? No. (taking the gum out of her mouth) I don't even know where this gum came from.
Frances dances behind her, mirroring her movements. Other pairs of dancers do the same. Colleen claps her hands:
Understudies, out!
Frances and a few others go to the side and watch the primary company dance.
INT. SMALL DANCE STUDIO. LATER.
Frances teaches ballet to a group of young girls.
INT. SUPERMARKET - LATE DAY
Frances shops.
INT. SUBWAY CAR - LATE DAY
Frances goes uptown. Clutching two huge grocery bags.
EXT. MIDTOWN CORPORATE BUILDING - EARLY EVENING
Frances waits outside a building, watching people exit. She sees Sophie.
AHOY SEXY! AHOY!
(laughing) AHOY SEXY!
EXT. BRYANT PARK - EVENING

Sophie and Frances eat. The park is almost deserted. It's twilight and too cold for a picnic. They have pushed several tables together and made a banquet table and spread everything out. They toast two Pellegrino bottles.
INT. SUBWAY - NIGHT
On the subway. Sophie keeps checking her phone. No service.
Sometimes there is, for a second... What is going on with that phone, lady?
I didn't want to do it without knowing it was okay with you first. Do what?

(putting her phone away) I want to move into this apartment with Phoebe. It's this great apartment in the West Village which is what I've always wanted and you know that but I don't want to do it if you're not okay with it -Oh --
But they need to know by tomorrow. So I kind of need to tell them something today...
Who have you been emailing?
My parents, for help with the broker's fee and stuff.
You have to pay OUR rent.
I know and I'll pay until the end of the lease but it's only for another two months anyway. Are you okay with it? I really don't want to do it if you're not okay with it.
I sort of thought we were going to renew the lease.
Yeah, but we never talked about it.
I could have moved in with Dan.
Not if you broke up.
That's why we broke up. Really? No.
FRANCES
It's LITERALLY on my favorite street. Phoebe said it was too good to pass up.
You hate Phoebe.
She's okay.
We make fun of her.
She said I'd always regret it if I don't just go for it.
Then go for it.
We're both really clean.
I said go for it.
I wish it was going to be with you. Yeah.
But Phoebe found the place. Right.
Is this train skipping DeKalb?
She gets up to look at the subway map.
We could look for another place together, if you want, but it's really hard to find stuff in the West Village.
I can't afford the West Village.
I know it's so expensive. My raise just barely covers the rent.
You should take the place.
I'm not leaving you - I'm just moving neighborhoods.
Frances's eyes well up.
Are you okay? Yeah.
Totally.
Frances is messing with her hand.
What are you doing?
I just put my ring on my thumb and I'm having trouble getting it off.
Hold your hand over your head. It'll drain the blood out. Frances does.
I look like I'm asking a question.
I'm not doing anything wrong. Our lease was going to be up in two months anyway.
They ride in silence, Frances' hand over her head.
INT. FRANCES AND SOPHIE'S APARTMENT. DAYS LATER. MORNING

Frances gets a tea bag out and puts it in a mug. She rattles around the cabinets looking for a kettle. Not finding a kettle, she boils water in a saucepan. While she's waiting for the water to boil, she lights a cigarette. The apartment is nearly empty. It turns out Sophie had most of the furniture and Frances had most of the books. It looks like the crack den of a pretty well educated junkie. The water boils. Frances tries to pour it into the mug, but scalds herself. She kicks the kitchen cabinet. Frances is on the phone. She moves.
(on the phone) We bought the kettle TOGETHER! REMEMBER? At the Mexican superstore? Jesus! BRING IT BACK.
OR BUY ME ANOTHER KETTLE.
INT. APARTMENT HALLWAY. DAY
Mailbox. Frances is checking the mail. Sees something she likes. Tax rebate! Whoops and runs out onto the street.
EXT. BANK. DAY. MOMENTS LATER
Frances leaves the bank. Is still elated. Looks around, unsure what to do next. Her elation leaves her slightly. She pulls out her phone and scrolls. Half shrugs, calls someone.
Hey I just got a tax rebate, want to go out to dinner?!
INT. RESTAURANT. NIGHT
Lev (from the party) is scrolling through his phone. He finds the photo he was looking for and hands the phone to Frances across the table.
Sophie said you liked basketball.
That was just one time. Frances changes the picture.
Wow! Crazy!
(looking at the picture) Oh, yeah, I was goofing around on this Vintage Triumph I think I'm going to buy.
Motorcycles are so loud. What?
I mean, you can't listen to music while you're on a motorcycle. But you're on a motorcycle.
Lev takes back his phone, finds a new picture.
He's such a dick.
I know, but don't you just love him?
The waitress comes up to give them the check. Frances grabs it and puts her credit card in.
Yes! NO!
No...I mean, YES! I'm the one with the tax rebate!
Can I take this? Yes! Oh...fine.
The waitress leaves. Just because you bought dinner doesn't mean I'm going to sleep with you.
(not getting it) I'm not trying to sleep with you...
No, I'm pretending to be a liberated woman.
Oh, got it.
Thanks for paying. That was very sweet. You're a lady.
He takes her hand. She lets him. The waitress comes back. Frances immediately withdraws her hand.
I'm sorry, but the card didn't go through, do you have another card you want to try?
Oh, shit! Sorry, yes, um, this is a debit card?
Only credit cards or cash.
Okay. (to Lev) I'm so embarrassed, I'm not a real person yet. I got it.
(getting up from the table) No, no, no, I'm just going to go run and get some cash.
EXT. STREETS AROUND THE RESTAURANT. NIGHT
Frances hustles down the street, looking for an ATM.
INT. DELI. NIGHT
Frances runs in.
Not working. No ATM.
EXT. STREETS AROUND THE RESTAURANT. NIGHT
She looks for a bank or something. She stops a woman.
Do you know where a bank is?
Um, there's a Bank of America about five blocks up.
EXT. BANK. NIGHT

Frances punches in her pin in the ATM area of a closed B of A while a homeless man looks on. "Do you accept the $3.00 service charge?" She sighs, her finger hovering over the button, accepts. As she passes the homeless guy:
Sorry, sorry...
EXT. STREETS AROUND THE RESTAURANT. NIGHT
Frances runs back to the restaurant. Falls. Hard. Gets up and keeps running.
INT. RESTAURANT. NIGHT
The restaurant is considerably more cleared out. Frances has been gone a long time. She sits down plunks the money down, happy. Lev is on the phone.
He hangs up.
Frances is flushed and out of breath.
No.
No, did you go to -- I mean, where were you? I'm sorry, I had so much trouble
finding -She casually picks up the last bit of her wine and downs it. She puts her glass down, and wipes at her face with the napkin. She looks at the napkin, it has streaks of red.
She tries to trace the blood up her arm to its source.
She feels her arm, trying to figure it out. Her elbow.
Shit. Sorry. Don't apologize to me. Oh, damn it. Oh, let me, I'm going to go to the bathroom, just one second.
INT. LEV AND BENJI'S APARTMENT. NIGHT.
Frances stands in their apartment. it. Totally enamored with
This place is pretty awesome.
Lev enters with a First Aid Kit.
He places the bandage on her arm.

Yeah, I'm going to have to find a place to live. I can't afford to stay there alone. I was kind of thinking Washington Heights...? (lighting a cigarette) The chick crashing in the little room might be moving to Portland next month.
Oh, that's good to know. (then) You smoke inside? Yeah, don't you? No. Want to?
He offers her a cigarette. Lights it for her with a special lighter.
INT. LEV'S ROOM. MOMENTS LATER.
Frances is looking at the framed pictures on the wall. They are old and yellowed.

They are quiet a little too long. Lev moves towards her and puts his hand on her shoulder. Frances glances at the hand, and instantly makes a face and a strange noise that is definitely negative. It's something that if she had thought about for one second more that she wouldn't have done. Lev moves away. They hear Benji come through the door. He has two girls with him. They make a lot of noise.
RANDOM GIRL #1 (O.S.)
Oh my GOD! This place is AMAZING! Benji's home.
INT. LEV AND BENJI'S APARTMENT. LIVING ROOM. A LITTLE

LATER. One girl dances to music. Lev plays cribbage with another girl. Flirting heavily with her - looking at her cards, teasing her. Benji and Frances are talking. Frances drinks a scotch out of a proper scotch glass.
I love dance. I dated a dancer in high school and her teacher said I could have really had a future in it. (joking but serious) You liar. He's a liar.
No I'm not! Ask anyone from school! (to Frances) I really to love dance. It's pretty great...I mean it's a hard world, but LEV Do us a dance! I feel weird...
Just one move.
Frances gets up does something quickly and sits back down.
That was GREAT! You should be in this music video my friend is working on. I'll hook you guys up.
(to herself) You guys are...like magic.
Is Noodle Champion still open? I'm starving. I don't think so...
All we have is...we have eggs. I want something PREPARED. And BROUGHT to me.
INT. LEV AND BENJI'S APARTMENT. KITCHEN. LATER
Frances has the stove going with a couple of items.
INT. LEV AND BENJI'S APARTMENT. LIVING ROOM. LATER
Frances serves everyone omelettes prettily. People compliment her and the food.
She does a little twirl of happiness.
INT. LEV AND BENJI'S APARTMENT. LIVING ROOM. LATER.
The other two girls leave. Kiss everyone on the cheek.
And I'll let you know about my show. Yes! Please! I love your hair.
Everyone's gone, Frances moves to the couch between Lev and Benji.
She basks in it.
(nods, half-hearted) I should probably go, too. Make a left and a left out of the building and the F is on the other side of the bridge on East Broadway.
You'll see a 99 Cent Store -FRANCES Okay, yeah. I should go. (beat, indicating the extra room) Is that chick really moving to Portland? Yeah.
Before you go, do another dance.
(this time eager)
She gets up and starts to dance. The boys love it.
EXT. CHINATOWN. DUSK.
Music swells. Frances runs through the streets of Chinatown, bomber jacket and backpack on. She's running, almost dancing through the streets. She bursts into the apartment.
INT. BEDROOM. NIGHT.

Frances is asleep. She hears people having sex. She opens her eyes, stirs. Closes her eyes. The two boys, Benji and Lev, run into the bedroom and jump on top of her bed, play-tickling her and body slamming her. It's morning.
INT. KITCHEN TABLE. LATER
Benji on his computer, smoking a cigarette. Still in a Tshirt and pajama bottoms. Frances on her computer, backpack on, ready to leave. Both drinking Folgers instant coffee.
What time did you get in last night?
Um, late. Like 1:00.
Why didn't you come in and say hi?
I'm sorry -- I thought you were asleep.
I wasn't asleep. I heard you come in.
Then you knew what time I got in?
(ignoring her question) I'm working on some sample skits for Saturday Night Live. (MORE)
I know a producer there who said I could probably get a job writing for them. Cool.
That's such a lie.
Lev has come out of his room. Fully dressed, bare feet. A girl, Nessa, trails after him. She's about twenty. Very thin. Not wearing pants, just underwear and a big tshirt.
(putting out her hand to shake) Hello. I'm Frances.
Lev and I were going to make bacon egg bagels. You want some?
Oh, no. Thanks though. I wanted to go to the Met and drop this laundry off and I need to deposit a check, a whole...Sunday planned.
INT. LIVING ROOM. LATER
Frances eats a very large egg bagel very quickly. So quickly that she nearly chokes. Her backpack is now off. She notices how much faster she's eating than everyone else. Slows down. Or tries to.
Why do you all live together?
Well, I knew Benji through Sophie
Where is Sophie these days?
Busy with work.
Which Sophie? Sophie Kozak?
Get out! I fucked her little brother!
Oh, yeah, you fucked Thomas?
You fucked who?
(to Frances) How do you know her?
We went to college together and we're...we were born together.
Oh you're that Frances. Who is Thomas?
Not really but that's what we say. (beat) Yeah, I'm that Frances.
You should know she speaks so highly of you.
Right. I mean, we're best friends. She's been to my house for Christmas three times.
Why doesn't she go to her house?
(annoyed) She's Jewish.
She was saying last week how much she loves you.
You saw her last week? When did you fuck her brother?
Where were you guys?
(to Lev) I met you last night. (then to Frances) We were at that restaurant Po.
Who were you fucking last week, Lev?
What...is that a good restaurant? Was she with...who was she with?
Me and Thomas and that guy she dates, Patch?
Double date.
And some girl. Esme.
Phoebe. Cunt.
Who were you fucking last week Frances?
(joking) I make love.
Frances: UNDATEABLE!
Nessa is thinking about something. Puzzled.
Yeah. Aren't you a lot older than Sophie?
No. We went to college together.
You seem older.
I'm a couple months older.
You seem a LOT older. But less, like, grown up. It's weird. You have an older face...
...but like you don't have your shit together.
INT. LIVING ROOM. LATER.
Frances lays on the couch, shoes off. Benji still on the computer. Lev is standing by the door, holding a motorcycle helmet.
(a little sadly) Nessa can't come from sex.
That's a crazy helmet. Got to. Motorcycle law. Oh, can you leave that rent check for me?
Yeah, yeah, sorry. I'll put it on your desk. It's for...$950? Nope. $1200.
I...I don't, I thought we said $950 when I moved in.
Nope. The whole space is $4000 but the little room is only $1200 But, I --
No, she's right. We said she could pay that much until she got more cash together.
Remember because when I do the Christmas show I'll make more because I'll be doing like ten shows a week... Oh right right.
So we're good with $950? That's okay? I'm really sorry...
He puts down his motorcycle visor.
No prob. Catch you on the flipside!
Lev leaves.
He leaves so easily...
Transportation is his thing. Bike, skateboard. He has a car, too.
I can't even get out of the house on my feet!
She is quietly dejected.
You know what Virginia Woolf book this reminds me of?
Frances: UNDATEABLE.
Frances laughs.
Wanna watch a movie? Now?
Yeah, let's do a movie afternoon.
I already wasted the whole day - I already feel bad about the day.
I think it's a great day. I ate an egg bagel that Lev's slut made me. I internet acquired three pairs of very rare Ray Bans. I'm doing
I thought we were both broke!
I caved and finally took a loan from my step dad. Bastard. I need a fucking job, though.
What about SNL?
Yeah, maybe. It's gone so downhill.
Goes to the bar. Looks at his watch. Pours himself a scotch.
Until then, it's after three. I can drink. Let's do something fun. We could go to a movie.
Movies are so expensive now...I should be saving so I can pay full rent.
I'm not going to force you.
(getting up, ready) I have to work out, at least. I cannot get fat.
Frances puts on shoes, etc. Benji drinks his scotch and starts going through his records to pick one.
Do I look old to you?
No. Yes. How old?
Older than I am? Older than twentyseven?
No. Twenty-seven is old, though.
CUT TO: It's dark out. Benji and Frances watch a movie. Benji smokes and eats an egg roll. Frances wolfs down a box of General Tso's Chicken. She realizes she's eating too fast - tries to slow down.
INT. BOY'S APARTMENT. NEW DAY
Sophie looks at the stuff. Frances bobs next to her.
Isn't this place AMAZING?
Is that an Eames chair?
Yeah, isn't this GREAT?!
Total rich-kid apartment.
The boys just have good eyes. They find stuff all the time.
Do you know who Lev Shapiro's dad is?
He doesn't get along with his dad...Lev and Benji are artists.
Exactly -- the only people who can afford to be artists in New York are rich.
I'm an artist. I'm not rich.
You are rare. (beat) It's just this apartment is very...aware of itself.
